behavior_summary: FTABLEEW::look runs when an actor 'looks' at the table object. It marks the item/process with an info flag and a process-exclude flag, constructs the literal string "table ", and requests the item to 'bark' that string to the viewing process.
The routine passes the viewer process id (inferred from the caller stack frame) to Item::bark so the viewer receives the description.
After barking the description the routine frees the temporary string, inspects the bark return value (used in an 'implies' check), then immediately suspends the script.
notable_control_flow: Early writes: set an item info flag (constant pushed) and then set a process-exclude flag for the object — these are write-side effects performed before producing the description.
Construct-call-cleanup sequence: allocate/prepare literal string "table ", call Item::bark with (viewer_pid, string), then free the string.

Post-call check: push return value from Item::bark, perform an 'implies' operation and pop into a temp/local before executing suspend — the exact behavioral effect of this check is inferred and not fully certain.
open_questions: Exact numeric id and semantic meaning of the 'info' flag written early (a numeric constant like 0x0208 is pushed in the code; its precise gameplay meaning is not certain).
Precise semantics of the 'process exclude' operation for this object (appears to prevent some further processing of the item but exact effect is inferred).
Meaning of the 'implies' operation on Item::bark's return value and how the popped result affects later logic — it is read then the script suspends, so its runtime impact is unclear.
Whether any other engine or UI-side side-effects accompany the bark (sound, animation) beyond the textual output.

main_function.name: U8P_FTABLEEW::look
main_function.purpose: Respond to an actor examining (looking at) the table by setting item/process state flags and emitting a short descriptive text ('table ') to the viewer, then suspend.
entry_conditions: Event 0 — invoked when an actor issues a 'U8P_FTABLEEW::look' (examine) action on the table object (inferred).

function_map.name: U8P_FTABLEEW::look
function_map.role: Handle the 'U8P_FTABLEEW::look' (examine) event for the table object: set item/process flags, construct and emit the description string, then suspend.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item routine invoked to present a short text message from an item to a target process/player (used to show 'table ').

external_calls.purpose: Deliver the description string ('table ') to the viewer process so the player sees the item's short description.
Free the temporary string buffer used to hold the literal 'table ' after barking it (inferred from code sequence).

writes.name: item info (constant pushed, inferred)
writes.likely_effect: Mark the item with a particular info/state key (engine-level), probably to note it has been examined or to alter future processing.

writes.name: process exclude
writes.likely_effect: Exclude this item/process from some kinds of processing or scheduling (prevents further automatic handling); used immediately after setting info.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: FTABLEEW::look runs when an actor 'looks' at the table object. It marks the item/process with an info flag and a process-exclude flag, constructs the literal string "table ", and requests the item to 'bark' that string to the viewing process.
